title: Dynamic Height Zoning: Game-Changer in Trade Show Design
excerpt: Transform your trade show booth with dynamic height zoning – boost visibility, engagement, and conversions using the 10-5-1 vertical strategy.

body.text: Have you ever wondered what makes certain booths at trade shows feel like they’re pulling you in, even from across the room? What’s their secret? Is it the flashy graphics? The innovative products? Or could it be something more subtle, something that taps into our human instincts and psychology?
Let me tell you: Dynamic Height Zoning is the answer. And here’s the kicker—it’s not just about how your booth looks; it’s about how your booth makes people feel and engage with it.

The truth is, in an ocean of exhibitors all vying for attention, standing out requires more than just good design—it demands strategic placement, creativity, and a deep understanding of human behavior. When you master the concept of height zoning, you create a booth experience that pulls people in, holds their attention, and ultimately leads to more conversions. Let’s explore how this game-changing concept can transform your trade show experience.
Understanding Visitor Sight Lines: Why Human Peripheral Vision Influences Engagement

Before we dive deep into the specifics of height zoning, let’s talk about how humans engage with their environment , especially in a crowded, fast-paced space like a trade show. If you’ve ever walked into a trade show, you’ve likely experienced that instant moment when your eyes naturally scan the space for something interesting . That’s not by chance. Our eyes are wired to look for movement and contrast , and when we’re in a crowded environment, we gravitate towards objects that break the monotony—like tall signs or dynamic visuals.
How Does Peripheral Vision Work in Crowded Spaces?

items: Attention-Grabbing from Afar : Our peripheral vision is tuned to spot large, prominent visuals, especially those that stand above the crowd. That’s why tall booths with strong branding elements naturally catch the eye , even from a distance.
Immediate Recognition : The human eye processes what’s directly in front of us and also scans from top to bottom, making the upper portion of your booth the prime real estate for drawing attention.

Overcoming Crowded Competition : At a trade show, you’re not just competing for attention; you’re competing for mental space . By using height strategically, you can ensure that your booth doesn’t just blend into the background.

body.text: The 10-5-1 Rule: Structuring Your Booth for Maximum Engagement

Let’s talk about the 10-5-1 rule —a simple but incredibly effective framework for maximizing engagement using height. Think of it as structuring your booth like a layered experience , where each height zone caters to visitors at different levels of engagement.
10-Foot Zone: Large Branding for Immediate Recognition
This is your prime real estate , where you create instant recognition and capture attention from afar. It’s the first thing that catches people’s eye when they enter the exhibit hall.

items: What to Include :
Huge Branding Elements : This could be oversized banners , high-reaching logos , or large, dynamic visuals that are visible from a distance.
Signature Products : Place your most iconic or flagship product at the top, where it will be noticed by anyone walking past. Think of this like a billboard for your brand.
Clear Messaging : Whether it’s a catchy slogan or a strong brand promise, this space is for getting your message across quickly and clearly .
Why It Works :
People will recognize your brand immediately , even if they’re far away. This zone maximizes your booth’s visibility and draws in traffic, especially in a sea of competing exhibitors.

body.text: 5-Foot Zone: Interactive Spaces for Hands-On Engagement
Once you’ve got them close, it’s time to engage them at a deeper level. The 5-foot zone is where the action happens—this is where your booth becomes an experience .

items: What to Include :
Interactive Displays : Use touchscreens , VR demos , or product samples for visitors to interact with your products.
Live Demonstrations : Set up areas where visitors can watch live demos of your products, or better yet, try them out for themselves.
Experiential Elements : From games to hands-on activities, this space invites visitors to actively participate in the booth, making them more invested in your offering.
Why It Works :

This is where the fun happens . When visitors engage with your product firsthand, they form a stronger connection. The 5-foot zone turns passive observation into active involvement , which is key to longer engagement and brand recall .

body.text: 1-Foot Zone: Private Conversation Spaces for Lead Nurturing

You’ve caught their attention, they’ve interacted with your product, and now it’s time to close the deal . The 1-foot zone is all about personal connection —this is where you have one-on-one conversations with your most promising leads.

items: What to Include :
Seated Areas for Discussions : Think about creating a comfortable lounge-style seating or small private meeting rooms where you can talk one-on-one with leads.
Consultation Stations : Offer tailored solutions or personalized advice based on the visitor’s unique needs.
Exclusive Offers : This is where you can seal the deal , whether through exclusive discounts , consultations , or special offers available only to visitors who engage in this space.
Why It Works :

When you get visitors into a more intimate setting , it’s easier to build trust and move them down the sales funnel . This zone is where you can have focused, high-quality interactions and nurture leads into becoming loyal customers.

body.text: Material & Lighting Integration: Elevating Your Booth with Design

Now that we’ve covered the structure of your booth, let’s talk about how materials and lighting can amplify the effects of your height zoning. These elements are crucial in guiding attention and creating the perfect atmosphere.
Translucent Materials for Smooth Height Transitions
One key aspect of dynamic height zoning is how the materials you use can affect how visitors experience different zones.

items: What to Include :
Frosted Glass or Acrylic : These materials can create a light, airy feel that encourages visitors to move from one zone to the next, without feeling overwhelmed by hard lines or sharp boundaries.
Flowing Design : Materials that allow light to pass through create a sense of continuity , making the height transition feel natural and seamless.
Why It Works :

The use of translucent materials helps create a smooth visual flow between different zones, making the booth feel more open and inviting . This helps guide visitors naturally from one area to another without feeling confined.

body.text: Gradient Lighting to Create Perception Depth
Lighting isn’t just functional; it can be used as a powerful tool to create depth, highlight features, and guide the flow of visitors.

items: What to Include :

Gradient Lighting : Gradually transition from bright, energetic lighting at the top of the booth to softer, ambient light as visitors move lower. This gives a sense of depth and perspective , drawing attention to key features.
Layered Lighting : Use lights at different heights and angles to create dimension and highlight specific products or areas.
Why It Works :

Lighting doesn’t just attract attention—it shapes the environment and influences how visitors perceive the space. A well-lit booth feels dynamic and engaging , guiding visitors towards areas that are important to your sales goals.

body.text: Suspended Elements to Capture Interest from Afar

Nothing grabs attention quite like something suspended in the air . Suspended elements , whether they are signs, lighting fixtures, or display items, are perfect for catching the eye of someone across the hall.
What to Include :

items: Hanging Displays or Banners : Large signs or floating structures can create a dramatic effect , making your booth visible from far away.
Suspended Lights : This can direct attention to specific features while also creating a vibrant atmosphere .
Why It Works :
Suspended elements add a sense of playfulness and intrigue , drawing attention to the upper levels of your booth and making your exhibit stand out among the sea of competitors.

body.text: The Success Formula: Boosted Dwell Time and Conversions

Booths that use dynamic height zoning have been shown to increase dwell time by 35% and boost lead conversions significantly. How? By creating a visually engaging, experience-driven booth that evolves as visitors move through different zones.
Here’s the formula for success:

items: Strategic Height Zones : Divide your booth into distinct zones based on the level of engagement—10-foot for visibility, 5-foot for interactivity, and 1-foot for personal connections.
Engagement with Experience : Ensure that visitors can interact, test, and engage with your products in a way that makes them feel involved.
Material & Lighting Mastery : Use translucent materials and gradient lighting to create a smooth, welcoming flow from one zone to the next, while also highlighting key features.
Lead Nurturing at Its Best : Make the 1-foot zone the heart of your sales efforts, turning potential leads into conversions through personalized interactions.

body.text: By using height zoning , you create a multi-dimensional experience that not only captivates but also converts . It’s a booth design that’s not just seen, but felt—and remembered long after the event.
Final Thoughts

Dynamic height zoning isn’t just a design technique; it’s a holistic strategy that taps into human instincts and creates an experience that resonates with visitors on a deeper level. The vertical game-changer isn’t just about standing out—it’s about creating engagement, interaction, and connections that lead to meaningful results.

At B2B Sales Arrow, we specialize in delivering trending tradeshow booth designs for international events that will make your brand stand out and drive results. From strategic booth design to on-ground coordination, we’re here to ensure your next event is a success.
Let us help you craft an unforgettable experience that converts attention into action. Contact us today to take your event strategy to the next level! Get in Touch with B2B Sales Arrow Now!

title: The Secret to Doubling Your Leads at Trade Shows: The "Golden Triangle" Booth Layout That Works   -
excerpt: Final Hour Success Story: How One Booth Outperformed the Entire Event Let’s rewind to one of the biggest international trade shows […]

body.text: Final Hour Success Story: How One Booth Outperformed the Entire Event Let’s rewind to one of the biggest international trade shows of 2025. Thousands of exhibitors had invested months in preparing elaborate booth setups, spending heavily on logistics, branding, and promotional materials.
But as the final hour approached, something unexpected happened.

While most booths started packing up, a select few were still buzzing with visitors. Our booth, in particular, saw a 4x increase in traffic and doubled their previous year’s leads—right when others were calling it a day.
What changed?

items: Better product? No.
A bigger marketing budget? Not at all.
More aggressive sales reps?

body.text: It was something far simpler yet incredibly powerful:
A Strategic Shift in Booth Design
Months of research, testing, and analyzing foot traffic behavior revealed a game-changing insight:
The problem isn’t just in how you market your booth—it’s in how you design it.
This led to the development of the “Golden Triangle” Layout—a scientifically-backed booth design that not only attracts more visitors but maximizes engagement and conversions.
What is The Golden Triangle Booth Layout?

The Golden Triangle Booth Layout is a precision-designed spatial strategy that use spatial psychology, behavioral mapping, and optimized flow to maximize engagement. By strategically placing key interaction points in a triangular configuration, this layout naturally guides visitors through the booth, enhancing visibility, dwell time, and lead capture. It’s a data-driven approach engineered to turn footfall into meaningful connections.
The Fatal Flaw in Conventional Booth Design: Psychological Barriers That Kill Engagement
Imagine this:
You’re walking down a busy trade show aisle, scanning the booths. Some have flashy banners, some have massive LED screens, but something makes you hesitate before stepping in.
This hesitation is the result of psychological barriers created by bad booth design—and they’re killing your engagement rates.
Why Traditional Rectangular Layouts Don’t Work

items: The Box Effect : Standard rectangular booths subconsciously feel like closed-off spaces—especially when there are tables or counters blocking the entrance. Visitors feel like they’re stepping into a mini-store or office, triggering hesitation.
Invisible Entry Barriers : If a booth’s structure looks too rigid, visitors assume they need an invitation to enter, reducing spontaneous interactions.

Limited Engagement Areas: Many traditional booths push product displays to the front and sales teams to the back—a major mistake. This forces sales reps to wait for visitors instead of actively engaging them.
Lack of Flow: The human brain prefers natural movement. A symmetrical booth with a single, straight-line pathway can feel restrictive, leading to lower booth traffic.

body.text: The Solution: The Golden Triangle Layout & Asymmetrical Balance
The 60-30-10 Rule for Space Optimization & Visitor Flow
The Golden Triangle Layout is built on the principle of asymmetrical balance, which leverages the 60-30-10 rule for better space utilization:
✔ 60% Open Engagement Space

items: No barriers. This area is fully open to naturally draw attendees inside.
Focuses on interactive elements like live demos, product trials, or AR experiences to keep people engaged.

body.text: ✔ 30% Focused Interaction Zone

items: A semi-private area where deeper conversations happen.
Includes consultation tables, video screens, or high-touch customer engagement stations.

body.text: ✔ 10% Conversion Zone

items: A clearly designated space for high-value leads.
Includes a VIP lounge, lead qualification station, or fast-track meetings for serious buyers.

body.text: Mapping Visitor Movement: High-Engagement Zones
Once a layout is optimized, the next step is understanding how visitors move inside the space. Heat mapping uses AI-powered sensors or manual tracking to analyze visitor behavior:
An example of heat mapping technology used in events is seen in concerts and exhibitions.

items: Where do visitors first stop?
Which areas are attracting the most attention?
Which sections are ignored or have dead space?

body.text: By tracking this data, exhibitors can optimize high-engagement areas and place key elements where they have the biggest impact.
How to Use Heat Mapping to Optimize Booth Design
Identify “Hot Zones” for Engagement

items: Place product demos, AR experiences, or interactive screens where people naturally linger.
Example: A cybersecurity firm at RSA Conference 2023 noticed attendees spent 60% more time at areas with hands-on activities vs. passive displays.

body.text: Remove “Cold Zones” That Kill Energy

items: If a part of your booth isn’t getting attention, redesign it or move key elements elsewhere.
Example: A fintech company at Money 20/20 used heat maps to discover that their best conversation spot was NOT at the booth’s entrance but near their interactive touchscreen stations.

body.text: Guide Traffic Flow Like a Retail Store

items: Major retail brands use subtle design cues (pathways, lighting, product placement) to steer customers toward high-impact areas—and the same principle applies to booths.

body.text: Material Selection for Seamless Visitor Movement
Once you have the right layout and engagement strategy, the next step is using materials strategically to guide movement naturally.
How Textural Transitions Improve Booth Experience
Humans subconsciously react to different textures underfoot. Smart exhibitors use this to control flow without physical barriers.

Example: A healthcare tech company at HIMSS 2024 used a gradient flooring transition (soft carpet at entry → sleek, polished tiles at the demo area) to subconsciously guide attendees toward their main engagement space. The result? 27% more demo signups.
ROI Impact: The Business Case for Smart Booth Design
Trade shows are expensive. Booth space, logistics, marketing, and staffing add up quickly. If your booth isn’t maximizing ROI
How the Golden Triangle Layout Increases ROI

items: 18-25% Higher Sales Conversions: Businesses using strategic booth design see a direct increase in deals closed on-site.
40% Longer Booth Visits: A well-designed layout keeps attendees engaged, increasing the chances of deeper conversations and conversions.
30-50% More Qualified Leads: Companies that optimize engagement zones and visitor flow report significantly higher lead capture rates.
Lower Marketing Waste: Instead of spending more on ads to drive traffic, exhibitors convert more of their existing foot traffic into leads and sales.

body.text: How to Apply This to Your Next Trade Show
Your Trade Show Booth Optimization Checklist:

✔ Is your booth layout inviting or restrictive? Avoid enclosed spaces. ✔ Are you using the 60-30-10 rule? Balance open space, engagement zones, and conversion areas. ✔ Do you have clear visitor flow paths? Use subtle design cues (flooring, lighting, pathways). ✔ Are you tracking visitor movement? Use heat maps or AI-driven analytics to optimize engagement areas. ✔ Is your engagement zone interactive? Demos, AR/VR, gamification—keep visitors engaged. ✔ Do you have a high-intent lead capture strategy? Identify and prioritize decision-makers.

B2B Sales Arrow is your ultimate partner for high-performance trade show booths that maximize impact. With 200+ booths delivered, we’ve helped brands transform their presence at top international events. Our innovative designs and strategic booth layouts have resulted in 4X more visitor engagement and increased lead conversions.
Trusted by billion-dollar clients, we specialize in delivering high-impact booths that drive real business results. Let’s build your high-conversion trade show booth design today!
Contact B2B Sales Arrow now and make your next event a game-changer.
Final Thought: Will Your Booth Be an Attraction or an Obstacle?

Most exhibitors invest heavily in marketing and branding but overlook the impact of strategic booth design. If your layout creates psychological barriers, has no visitor flow strategy, and wastes valuable engagement areas, you’re leaving qualified leads on the floor.
The Golden Triangle Layout isn’t just a design change—it’s a conversion strategy.
So, the real question is: Will you continue with a rigid, traditional setup, or are you ready to transform your booth into a high-engagement, lead-generating powerhouse?

title: Looking for a Booth Design Strategy That Converts? Try the Silent Seller Approach  -
excerpt: A Visual-First Trade Show Strategy for Event Success  What if your tradeshow booth could turn heads and drive sales without […]

body.text: A Visual-First Trade Show Strategy for Event Success

What if your tradeshow booth could turn heads and drive sales without anyone speaking? That’s the power of a well-planned booth design strategy one that uses visual storytelling and visual merchandising techniques to convert passive interest into real action.

Strategic visual placement makes your tradeshow booth design stand out, drawing crowds and maximizing participation. Amid a sea of eye-catching tradeshows, real distinction demands more than flashy setups.
Creating a dynamic booth design strategy requires balancing branding, engagement, and valuable audience data. Remember, all of these play a crucial role in shaping a strong event success strategy .
Why the Silent Seller Strategy is Key to Post-Event Success?

With the silent seller strategy, data isn’t wasted – it’s turned into future possibilities. The “Silent Seller” strategy is all about understanding and applying deep data insights to encourage purchases without disrupting the customer’s journey.

When you have a compelling booth design strategy , it enhances lead generation and boosts visitor interaction. For example, by using digital surveys, engaging kiosks, and interactive displays, attendees are eager to share insights. Once the event wraps up, this data helps shape smarter strategies and stronger lead nurturing.
How Visual Elements Drive Sales Before Words Come into Play?

Did you know 90% of the information processed by the brain is visual? In the middle of tradeshow chaos, decision-makers depend on quick visuals to make fast choices. That’s where a smart tradeshow booth strategy comes into play; using the right colors, lighting, and visuals to grab attention before a single word is spoken.
Motion-Triggered Content

items: Incorporating movement into your booth design strategy ensures higher engagement and longer visitor interaction.
Interactive features like motion sensors and touch panels create engaging visuals that showcase your brand.
Dynamic content reacts to movement, turning booth displays into engaging, brand-centered experiences.

body.text: Negative Space

items: Designing booths with open space, not clutter, keeps your message strong and visually striking.
Keeping 40% of your layout clear ensures attendees focus on vital brand elements and messaging.
A strong booth presence reassures leadership and keeps attendees interested, building valuable relationships.

body.text: Progressive Disclosure

items: Successful tradeshow booths draw attendees in with visuals, then reveal valuable product details over time.
With this tradeshow strategy , leaders hold attention while guiding audiences through information at a steady pace.
Progressive disclosure helps trade show brands guide visitors, qualify leads, and showcase customer-first values.
The approach facilitates data collection, as attendees interacting with deeper content layers are likely more qualified leads.
Presenting details progressively not only captures attention but also highlights the brand’s dedication to a seamless and customer-friendly journey.

body.text: Color Psychology in Sales: The Silent Influencer
Colors have a powerful influence, shaping emotions and perceptions in ways we barely notice. Color psychology in your tradeshow booth design creates an environment of trust, energy, or urgency.

We at B2B Sales Arrow recognize how vibrant colors bring tradeshow booths to life. Our booth designs pull in attendees, ensuring your brand stays memorable and encourages engagement. Do you know that choosing a bold green backdrop can showcase a tech company’s dedication to innovation and progress?
Typography & Readability in Booth Design: A Strategic Approach for Tradeshows

Standing out at a tradeshow is tough. Clear, bold typography plays a crucial role in any tradeshow strategy by helping your booth grab attention instantly. The right fonts not only make your brand stronger but also make your message easy to read.

Make your trade show booth stand out with bold contrast, easy readability, and smart organization; key elements of a successful tradeshow strategy . Helvetica and Arial are great sans-serif choices for a clean, modern style that improves readability.

Fonts speak volumes, example, bold uppercase exudes power, while rounded letters create a warm, approachable vibe. The right font choice enhances booth recognition and strengthens brand identity, fostering real engagement.
Large-Format Display Principles for High Visibility
Use high-contrast colors like black and white to ensure your trade show display grabs attention. Make your message clear with a bold headline or call-to-action to avoid any confusion.

items: Pick fonts that stay sharp at any size, ensuring at least 3-inch height for visibility from 10 feet.
Less is more—use brand elements wisely to maintain focus and maximize your design’s impact.

body.text: Differences Between Static and Dynamic Text Placements for Lead Engagement
Museum Exhibit Strategy: A Blueprint for Effective Tradeshow Booth Design

Museums excel at guiding visitors through a structured narrative, and your tradeshow booth can do the same. An effective booth design strategy divides the space into zones – awareness, consideration, and decision-making. It guides visitors through a seamless journey that naturally leads to conversions.
First impressions matter. A bold demo draws them in, a curated showcase keeps them engaged, and a private space seals the deal.
Learning from Museums: Creating a Guided Customer Journey

items: Narrative-Oriented Design – Use storytelling to build emotional connections.
Interactive Engagement – Incorporate live demos, quizzes, or augmented reality.
Guided Pathways – Use signage and lighting to direct attendees toward key messages.

body.text: Looking to Leave a Lasting Impression at Your Next Tradeshow?
Let’s be real—most trade show booth design look great… but don’t do much else. People walk by, maybe grab a flyer or a piece of candy, nod politely, and keep it moving. Sound familiar?
That’s where B2B Sales Arrow flips the script with a focused booth design strategy tied directly to your event success strategy .

Instead of just setting up a flashy display, we help you build a booth that sells. We’re talking about turning your space into a lead-magnet-slash-conversion-machine by integrating smart, proven B2B sales tactics into every inch of the setup. It’s not rocket science—it’s just about aligning visuals with your message, so the right people stop, listen, and engage.

Our tradeshow strategy puts conversations first, the kind that solve real problems and stick with decision-makers. By clearly showing the value of your product or service (without buzzwords), your booth becomes more than eye candy – it becomes the entry point to a deal.

Whether you’re a solo creator chasing B2B partnerships or a full team aiming to make a splash, our event success strategy turns your booth into more than a backdrop—it becomes your business generator.
Step up your tradeshow game today. Make every interaction count. Make a bold statement: B2B Sales Arrow designs booths that sell!
Summary
Objective of the blog: To explain how strategic visual hierarchy in tradeshow booths can attract and engage visitors without direct sales pitches, boosting lead generation and brand recall.

The Silent Seller Strategy is all about making your tradeshow booth grab attention and win sales without hard selling. Instead of relying on sales pitches, it uses smart visuals—like eye-catching colors, moving displays, and clean layouts—to pull people in.

The trick is to keep 40% of the space open (no clutter) and share information bit by bit to keep visitors curious. Interactive screens and surveys make the experience fun while collecting useful customer data.

Colors play a big role—red creates excitement, blue builds trust—and big, bold signs make sure your message stands out. Taking inspiration from museums, the booth tells a story that guides visitors naturally toward buying. B2B Sales Arrow uses these ideas to design booths that quietly attract, engage, and convert leads—no talking needed.

title: How to Boost On-Ground Event Sales Qualified Leads by 100% Through Active Prospecting -
excerpt: Are you struggling to maximize the value of on-ground lead generation in events like trade shows and conferences? Did you […]

body.text: Are you struggling to maximize the value of on-ground lead generation in events like trade shows and conferences? Did you know that for 68% of businesses, lead generation is the top reason for participating in these events?

These platforms provide an invaluable opportunity for face-to-face interactions with potential clients to showcase products and services. However, simply attending isn’t enough. Have you ever considered the strategic approach of active prospecting? This method of proactively seeking and engaging potential leads can result in an increase in high-quality sales-qualified leads.
According to Statista , 84% of C-suite executives believe in-person events are crucial for their company’s success?
For instance, at the recent Tech Expo 2023, companies that employed active prospecting strategies saw a 30% increase in sales-qualified leads (SQLs) compared to passive exhibitors.

items: By actively engaging potential clients and services, businesses can not only capture attention but also convert interactions into meaningful sales opportunities.

body.text: In this comprehensive guide, we will delve into strategies and best practices to boost on-ground event SQLs by 100% through effective active prospecting.
What is Active Prospecting?

Active prospecting involves an intelligent and proactive pursuit of potential customers in industry events. Unlike passive methods that wait for leads to come to you, active prospecting requires identifying potential leads, initiating contact, and nurturing these prospects towards a sale.

items: It’s a hands-on approach that demands thorough preparation, clear communication, and a strategic follow-up plan.
The goal is to connect with high-quality prospects who are most likely to convert into customers.

body.text: The Importance of Active Prospecting in On-Ground Events

On-ground events are an invaluable opportunity for businesses to connect with their target audience. These events gather a concentrated group of industry professionals, decision-makers, and potential clients thus it gives the opportunity of new account hunting.
Active prospecting at these events offers several benefits:

items: Increase Revenue, Improve Efficiency
Higher quality leads
Increased conversion rates
Better understanding of customer needs
Cost-effective marketing
Loyalty and repeat business
Positive brand perception
Stronger customer retention
Improved customer satisfaction
Enhances product development
Better market positioning
Competitive advantage
Customer-centric approach
Reduced sales cycle length
Increased overall revenue
Improved ROI
Enhanced customer acquisition

body.text: Strategies to Boost SQLs by 100% Through Active Prospecting

Achieving a 100% increase in SQLs through active prospecting at on-ground events involves a combination of preparation, strategic engagement, and diligent follow-up. Here’s how to implement these steps effectively:
1. Pre-Event Preparation

a. Pinpoint Your Ideal Attendees: Identify the key decision-makers and influencers attending the event. Use tools like event registration lists, LinkedIn, and industry-specific databases to gather information. For example, if your company offers cybersecurity solutions, target CISO, CTO, CIO, IT managers, and compliance officers who may face cybersecurity challenges.

b. Set Clear Objectives: Establish measurable goals for the event, such as the number of SQLs to generate or specific client segments to target. For instance, aim to secure meetings with at least 80 potential clients interested in cybersecurity during the event.

c. Train Your Team: Equip your team with in-depth knowledge about your products, services, and the unique selling propositions (USPs) that differentiate your offerings. Train them on how to engage in meaningful conversations and ask probing questions to qualify leads.
d. Create Event based Collaterals

E. (Leverage Event Marketing): Create pre-event buzz through targeted email campaigns, social media posts, and blog content. Highlight what visitors can expect at your booth, such as product demos, exclusive offers, or expert consultations. For example, a cybersecurity firm might offer a free network vulnerability assessment for attendees who visit their booth.
2. On-Site Active Prospecting Techniques

a. Booth Design and Engagement: Design an eye-catching booth that attracts attendees. Use interactive elements like touch screens, VR experiences, or live product demonstrations to draw in the crowd. Ensure your booth staff is proactive in engaging passersby, greeting them with friendly and inviting openers.
Example: A software company might showcase their latest product through an interactive demo, allowing attendees to experience the software’s features firsthand.

b. Lead Qualification Process: Implement a systematic lead qualification process using criteria like BANT (Budget, Authority, Need, Timeline). Train your staff to ask targeted questions that help identify the most promising leads.

Example: For a company offering project management software, booth staff might ask, “What project management challenges are you currently facing?” and “What solutions are you considering to address these challenges?”
c. Personalized Engagement: Tailor your pitch to the specific needs of each prospect. Use insights from your initial conversations to offer personalized solutions and relevant case studies.

Example: An event management company could highlight how they successfully planned and executed a large corporate gala, coordinating every detail from venue selection and catering to entertainment and logistics.

d. Real-Time Data Collection: Utilize technology to capture lead information efficiently. This can include digital forms, badge scanning, or mobile apps. Ensure accurate and detailed data collection for follow-up.
e. Host Mini-Sessions or Workshops: Offer short presentations or workshops at your booth to educate attendees about industry trends, best practices, or the benefits of your products.

Example: A company specializing in B2B lead generation could host a workshop on “Maximizing Lead Quality and Conversion Rates,” offering valuable insights into data-driven strategies for targeting and engaging potential clients.
3. Post-Event Follow-Up

a. Timely Follow-Up: After the event, follow up with leads promptly. Personalize your communication based on your interactions at the event. Mention specific points discussed to remind them of the value proposition.

Example: “It was great discussing your company’s data security challenges at the event. As promised, here’s the case study on how our solutions helped a similar company reduce their breach incidents by 40%.”
b. Nurture Leads with Relevant Content: Provide prospects with valuable content that addresses their specific needs and pain points. This could include whitepapers, webinars, or product comparisons.

c. Measure and Analyze: Assess the effectiveness of your active prospecting strategy by analyzing key metrics such as the number of SQLs generated, conversion rates, and overall return on investment (ROI). Use this data to refine your approach for future events.
Use Case
Case Study Client Success at Money 20/20: Key Achievements and Active Prospecting with B2B Sales Arrow

At Money 20/20, a leading global event in the finance and technology sector, the client sought to enhance brand visibility and generate high-quality leads. Partnering with B2B Sales Arrow, the client implemented an active prospecting strategy that resulted in remarkable outcomes.
Key Achievements

1. Digital Giveaways Wowed Attendees B2B Sales Arrow designed a digital giveaway campaign featuring exclusive content and software trials. Attendees accessed these through QR codes, which increased booth traffic and provided valuable lead data.

2. Interactive Booth Impressed Key Decision-Makers The client’s booth featured advanced interactive displays and live product demonstrations. This setup facilitated direct engagement with decision-makers, showcasing the client’s innovative solutions and enhancing brand perception.

3. Customized Lead Nurturing in Place Post-event, a customized lead nurturing campaign was launched. This included personalized emails and exclusive content, maintaining engagement and driving conversion of leads into sales-qualified opportunities.

4. 300+ Prospects Generated Active prospecting strategies, including booth interactions and digital campaigns, resulted in the collection of contact information from over 300 prospects, expanding the client’s sales pipeline.

6. Global Exposure Enhanced B2B Sales Arrow’s strategic execution at Money 20/20 significantly increased the client’s global exposure. Engagement with international media and influencers extended the client’s reach into new markets.
Conclusion

Active prospecting services at industry events is essential for generating high-quality sales-qualified leads. Ready to grow your event strategy? Partner with B2B Sales Arrow for expert active prospecting solutions that maximize your ROI. Contact us today to learn how we can help you achieve ×2 success at your next events!
Frequently asked questions

items: What is active prospecting, and how does it differ from traditional lead generation methods at events?

body.text: Active prospecting involves proactively engaging potential leads through direct interactions and personalized outreach, unlike traditional methods that rely on passive engagement, like booth displays. This hands-on approach results in higher-quality leads and better engagement.

items: How can we identify high-potential leads during an event?

body.text: Identify high-potential leads by conducting pre-event research, using event apps to filter attendees by criteria like job title or industry, and engaging with those showing interest in relevant topics during sessions and networking areas.

items: What are the best practices for engaging potential leads on the event floor? Engage leads effectively by having a well-trained team initiate meaningful conversations, using value-driven icebreakers, offering demonstrations, and asking open-ended questions to understand their needs.
How can we effectively follow up with leads after the event to ensure they convert into sales-qualified leads?

body.text: Follow up within 24-48 hours with personalized messages, referencing specific event conversations, offering additional resources like demos or white papers, and scheduling follow-up meetings to discuss their needs.

items: What metrics should we track to measure the success of our active prospecting efforts?

body.text: Track metrics such as the number of leads generated, conversion rate to sales-qualified leads, engagement levels post-event, impact on sales pipeline growth, and overall ROI from the event.

title: 5 Powerful Reasons Businesses Must Invest in Video Marketing -
excerpt: A leading American female hygiene brand cashes in on a stereotypical phrase in its “Like a Girl” campaign to grab […]

body.text: A leading American female hygiene brand cashes in on a stereotypical phrase in its “ Like a Girl ” campaign to grab audiences’ attention. The drive delivers an empowering message with realistic portrayals of what it means to run, throw, and fight “like a girl”. The video was widely acclaimed and won an Emmy, a Cannes Grand Prix award, and the Grand Clio award. Conversely, a generic text advertisement might fail to garner the same response considering the chances of capturing the viewer’s attention are much higher when videos are used. Studies show that audiences retain 95% of a video’s message compared to 10% when reading text.

With the rapidly evolving multimedia industry, marketers invest in various video formats, including explainers, product demos, social media, and educational videos, to engage the target audiences and drive measurable results directly linked to the organisation’s bottom line.

Videos are an excellent medium to strongly influence the audience’s perception of the marketing brand and help them gain a deeper understanding of products and services. A Hubspot survey found that 54% of audiences are interested in watching more video content from their favourite brands.

Video marketing aims to make the best use of videos to promote products or services, increase website traffic, and drive social media engagement. However, the multimedia format doesn’t limit to social media strategy. Marketing brands can leverage multiple distribution channels like landing pages, outbound email marketing, and ad campaigns to maximise their reach and engagement.
This blog highlights some of the most pertinent reasons businesses must consider video marketing for a successful marketing campaign:
1. Videos Boost Sales Conversions

Video advertising is one of the most effective ways to reach the business’s targeted prospects and generate quality leads. The audience must know your brand and offerings to boost sales conversions. Engaging videos with impactful content will pique the audience’s interest and explain the most complex concepts helping them make an informed buying decision. Moreover, incorporating video on landing pages can increase conversion rates by 80% .
2. Videos Improve ROI

According to Hubspot , 78% of internet users view online videos weekly, and 55% watch online videos daily. Incorporating various video formats into marketing strategy at every sales funnel stage is an excellent way to increase ROI.

Explainer videos raise brand awareness and assist the target audience in understanding how your solutions can be used to solve their problems. Creating a video testimonial of existing customers sharing their experiences is another great way to influence your audience’s perception of the brand.
3. Videos are SEO-friendly

Achieving a higher ranking for a business website page on Google is challenging. With billions of search queries daily, Google is the world’s largest search engine platform, followed by YouTube. Videos effectively improve a website’s ranking on search engine results pages (SERPs). However, as YouTube results are often featured on Google’s front page, leveraging the platform to upload videos increases the website’s rankings on the search engine.
Additionally, adding relevant keywords in the title, writing a persuasive description, and using trending hashtags in videos can further enhance your SEO efforts.
4. Videos Enhance Brand’s Visibility

Enhancing brand visibility is an essential step to increasing customer sales. Video marketing is an effective strategy for raising brand visibility and attracting potential customers. According to the Cisco Annual Internet Report , online videos will account for 82% of all internet traffic by 2022.

With the growing popularity of Instagram Reels and Tiktok, short-video content is garnering more eyeballs than ever. As short visuals become a leading medium to communicate messages, creating bite-sized videos and commercials that grasp the audience’s attention in lesser time can enhance your brand’s reach and engagement rate.
5. Videos Build Trust

Highly-engaging video content that drives meaningful conversations with the audience increases the time visitors spend on your social media profiles and invokes an emotional connection with the brand.

Marketing brands can utilise client testimonials to build credibility as it acts as social proof and humanises the brand. Additionally, demo videos or product tutorials add to the audience’s understanding of your offerings, allowing them to connect with the brand and establish trust.
Power Your Marketing Strategy With Captivating Videos

A solid marketing strategy is crucial for the brand’s success and must be planned with well-defined milestones and objectives. Compelling videos are a powerful tool in a marketing strategy, from raising brand awareness to increasing sales conversions. B2B Videos editing services convey messages to educate, inspire, and bridge the gap between the marketing brand and the audience. With the right goals and a clear understanding of the video formats that fit the marketing strategy, organisations can easily take their B2B marketing campaign to the next level and engage a larger audience.

title: 5 Steps To Build An Accurate Database For B2B Marketing Success -
excerpt: Your most lucrative marketing propositions packed with game-changing products or services will, plausibly, fail even to receive an acknowledgment if […]

body.text: Your most lucrative marketing propositions packed with game-changing products or services will, plausibly, fail even to receive an acknowledgment if they do not reach the most pertinent audiences, including Key Decision-makers from your target organizations. Ironically, around 42% of sales leaders deem a lack of access to reliable B2B contact data a pivotal impediment to effectuating an acute outreach campaign.

B2B databases primarily consist of data points such as contact details, functions, and designations of C-level executives from target accounts. Marketing teams leverage the data bank to calibrate various sales and marketing activities, including developing an Ideal Customer Profile (ICP), demand generation, lead generation, outbound sales, market research services, analytics, etc.

A chart-topping B2B marketing campaign largely depends on a high-performing outreach campaign designed to establish a direct connection with the CXOs from the companies most likely to reap the benefits of your brand offerings. Subsequently, the efficacy of the outreach campaign is directly proportional to the quality of the B2B database marketing teams gain access to. Consequently, B2B marketing imperatives necessitate organizations to make strategic investments in curating custom databases of accurate B2B contacts that perfectly match their Ideal Customer Profile.
This blog highlights key strategies organizations must execute to develop accurate databases containing reliable, actionable, and compliant data essential for B2B marketing success:

items: Define Marketing Objectives : Invest some time to clearly define the main objectives of the B2B databases before starting to develop one, whether the organization is keen on expanding its presence in an existing market or putting out feelers before entering a new one. Your marketing objectives will dictate the database structure, size, and development protocols.

Develop Your Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) : Marketing teams should develop a detailed persona of the prospect organizations (industry, size, revenue, geography) that could benefit from your offerings along with the key decision-makers or key influencers (job title, business function, seniority, etc.) your sales teams would be keen to connect and engage in conversations with.

Compliance : If you choose to in-house or outsource your database augmentation, ensure that the third-party agency aligns with the region-specific data compliance regulations such as GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) across Europe, CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) in California, PIPEDA (The Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act) in Canada, etc.

Choose The Right Data Source : Count on the right sources to acquire high-quality data for your B2B sales and marketing campaigns, based on your organization’s objectives, business requirements, and marketing priorities.

Partner With A Reliable B2B Data Provider : This is arguably the most common and the most efficient option for building a B2B database if you want to gain quick access to a bulk of prospects. You can partner with a reliable Global Contact Discovery Solution provider to procure a comprehensive, up-to-date, and highly accurate B2B database, including direct-dial phone numbers and verified email addresses of the prospects from your target market. The third-party vendors empower you with a large quantity of contact information and various attributes related to firmographics, technographics, intent data, account data, and market research services , which will help you refine your search.
Develop a B2B Database In-house : A cost-effective approach, manual data scavenging relates more to startups, small B2B organizations, and those with minimal financials.

body.text: There is a high likelihood of your prospects having a social media account on multiple platforms, including leading professional networking sites. Additionally, the websites of your target organizations are a great source of B2B data. Websites such as these typically feature the latest news releases, the story of their brand journey, and the ‘Meet The Team’ section.

Take abundant precautions to avoid poor data quality as you would be relying heavily on the internet and manual data collection programs. You must perform data cleansing and validation exercises in addition to data collection. The process will likely consume a significant amount of time and other resources.

items: Develop High-Quality Gated Content : Invest in creating highly relevant and thought-provoking content that helps you connect with your target audiences. You can trade your users’ contact information with high-quality gated content such as e-books, blogs, whitepapers, podcasts, and webinars.

body.text: Though a cost-efficient option for building your contact database, the option requires an upfront time investment. Opt-in lists are curated much slower and may contain user-generated errors. The efforts tend to snowball over time and yield healthy returns on your assets in the form of qualified leads for your sales team.

items: Data Validation and Quality Check : Performing stringent quality checks on your database will enable you to achieve accurately updated and well-structured data. While an excel data scan can identify duplicate entries, email list cleaning services leverage cutting-edge tools to track down fake email addresses, and spam traps and remove incomplete or inaccurate domain names. Additionally, subscriptions to renowned online databases support data validation exercises to ensure sales and marketing teams receive correct and accurate information as B2B databases decay each year.

body.text: Summing Up

Organizations choose the most appropriate strategic option for developing their sales and marketing databases based on their objective, size, and constantly evolving marketing preferences. The increasing competition in the B2B marketing landscape requires you to leverage high-quality contact data to deliver your key marketing messages to targeted prospects, ensuring a steady stream of highly-qualified leads to strengthen your sales success further. If implemented effectively, the strategy discussed above can arm sales and marketing teams with accurate B2B database information that is correct, verified, and compliant.

title: Which B2B Event Format Will be The Most Popular In 2022? -
excerpt: In the pre-Covid era, the majority of the businesses preferred hosting in-person seminars, trade shows, conferences and exhibitions, while events […]

body.text: In the pre-Covid era, the majority of the businesses preferred hosting in-person seminars, trade shows, conferences and exhibitions, while events in the digital spectrum were rare. However, during the pandemic, the events industry has witnessed a significant shift from the physical to virtual and hybrid.

The inevitable shift from in-person to virtual events in the B2B marketing space was, anyway, destined to be the future of corporate industry events. According to events industry experts, Virtual Events would have taken five-six years to gain widespread popularity instead of just two if it wasn’t for the pandemic.

This blog discusses the possibilities of a surprising future of the events industry that will thrive on Virtual, Hybrid, or In-person events: The Option Of Virtual We all are aware of the challenges posed by in-person events. Long haul travel, logistical challenges, overwhelming exhibition venues full of businesses competing to capture your attention. In simple terms, in-person events, particularly large-size gatherings, can be incredibly stressful for attendees. From the events analytics perspective, the power of virtual events packed with unmatched benefits is irrefutable. It is nearly impossible to imagine a future without the digital landscape of B2B events and rare to meet business leaders who do not acknowledge the stand out benefits of online meetings. The scaled-up transition to Metaverse, the groundbreaking innovation in the events industry, the capabilities to reach a global audience, high in-demand keynote speakers, and increased monetisation opportunities significantly improve Virtual Events’ marketability. Additionally, the digital phenomenon proves to be a highly cost-effective solution posing zero health and safety concerns. While a repurposed content marketing strategy propels future lead generation campaigns, post-event analytics is instrumental in developing a solid lead nurturing process. Going ahead into the future with virtual events is the only practical option for several brands. However, suppose you’re sceptical about virtual-only experiences. In that case, you’re in the same league as several other business leaders who face the dilemma if they should waive off the in-person component entirely, given a large number of attendees prefer to engage and learn best in-person. In-Person Events Continue As The Most Effective Option Over the next few years, depending on the health and public safety regulations, certain businesses will most likely return to hosting full-fledged in-person events. According to Brittani Dinsmore, Head of Marketing at Moz , the need for face to face interactions inspires 16% of the marketers to decrease their investments in Virtual Events in 2022. Screen fatigue is inevitable, and many attendees face burnout due to conducting multiple virtual meetings. Remote participation does not allow attendees to experience the full scope of some events, such as networking or a convention. The energy radiated at live events is unmatched and cannot be replicated in the virtual world. There’s a strong rationale that augments the importance of in-person interactions. In addition to the benefit of face to face connection, more often, it is much easier to generate highly relevant leads and attract new sponsors in person. For example, body language aids in 55% impact of our communication . Thus, it can be challenging to discern the nuances in body language in a virtual environment, leading to the loss of some meaningful connection in a virtual space. The events industry is likely to witness a gradual shift back to in-person events. With the new variants of Covid emerging, organisations will continue to host hybrid events allowing attendees the flexibility to choose between in-person attendance or remote participation. The Twin Powers Of Hybrid With a hybrid event strategy, you’re offering solutions to the needs of two varying subsets of audiences. Several enthusiasts are keen on highly engaging in-person event experiences, while some prefer to participate remotely. Hence, a hybrid event is an irresistible option if you encounter the dual nature of audience preferences, namely in-person and virtual. The USP of the hybrid model allows scaling up your events while enabling you to connect with attendees who cannot join the event in person due to travel restrictions, budget constraints or health and safety concerns. The complex setup of hybrid events demands an effective virtual platform and in-person meetings. Navigating the increased complexity, costs, and risks linked with integrating different platforms and technologies is crucial to organising a successful hybrid event. Re-engineering The Right Event Mix

Marketing organizations hold the ultimate choice of Virtual, Hybrid, or In-person events. With each event model offering a unique set of large-scale benefits, the future will likely see a mix of the three event types in the B2B events domain. The marketing leaders, often in collaboration with a corporate event management company , are responsible for optimizing the right combination of the three-event formats, depending on the audiences’ needs, preferences, and the marketing priorities of the organizations.

title: Lead Nurturing: Key To Maximizing Your ROI -
excerpt: Marketing organizations make significant investments in various lead generation strategies to acquire leads which are mere possibilities bearing no sale […]

body.text: Marketing organizations make significant investments in various lead generation strategies to acquire leads which are mere possibilities bearing no sale guarantee. Market research reports that more than 79% of the new prospects never convert into sales due to missing brand trust and lack of product understanding, creating a greater need for a strong lead nurturing program.

While demand generation focuses on capturing leads and stimulating the potential clients’ interest in the organization’s offerings, a successful lead nurturing campaign bridges the gap between a generated opportunity and a closed sale. A robust lead nurturing strategy focuses marketing and communication efforts on listening to the prospects’ needs and communicating practical solutions to their critical business challenges, establishing brand authority & expertise, and maintaining the connection until the possibilities are ready to make a purchase decision.
Improved Conversion Rates

According to Forrester Research, companies that implement a laser-focused lead nurturing strategy generate 50% more sales-ready leads at a cost, 33% less than the non-nurtured leads. The findings suggest an improved ROI when marketers focus on nurturing each lead via customised outreach.
Enhanced AOV (Average Order Value)

The Annuitas Group research on B2B marketing trends reveals that nurtured leads make purchases 47% larger than non-nurtured leads. With a conservative nurture strategy that incorporates relevant and targeted communication, marketing teams seamlessly enhance the organisation’s product value, assist the prospects in identifying the offerings’ ROI, and ways prospects’ teams can leverage the solutions to streamline daily business processes.
Reduced CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) For Improved ROI

Significant improvement in lead quality and enhanced AOV drive marked reduction in CAC to cast more comprehensive business benefits. With improved business processes, lead nurturing assists businesses in client base expansion at zero additional costs. According to the Forrester research, the process has a proven track record of improving the CAC by 33%. In other words, for every three currently acquired clients, lead nurture could benefit the business with an additional client at no extra cost.
Create Client Loyalty

Retaining current and past clients is equally essential as new client acquisition for business expansion. Organisations struggle to consistently meet their targets if new clients quickly switch over to the competition. Lead nurturing helps sales teams close deals with an awareness that the newly acquired clients offer a high customer lifetime value. Nurtured leads translate into clients keen on maximising their new purchases, thus making the new clients loyal and highly valuable.
Establish Industry Leading Authority

Several businesses struggle to gain a standout market identity and position their brand offerings as market leaders. While lead nurturing may not be the sole solution, developing an informative and trustworthy relationship and guiding each prospect towards a solution-driven sale drastically enhances the buyers’ perception of a brand’s authority.
Summing Up

Every business aspires to top the client’s search list of specific products or services. A robust lead nurture strategy, combined with sales qualified lead generation services , is the key to prospects receiving an advanced experience, increasing word of mouth to encourage earned media, and positioning the brand as the obvious choice for the target audience. The more nurturing the leads receive, the better they nurture the business market, eventually contributing to growth and an improved ROI.

title: 4 Must-Dos while Acquiring the C-Suite for your Virtual Events -
excerpt: With hundreds of emails inundating the inboxes of C-Suite executives, acquiring them as delegates for a virtual event is a […]

body.text: With hundreds of emails inundating the inboxes of C-Suite executives, acquiring them as delegates for a virtual event is a tough proposition. The most sought-after league of decision-makers is always time-strapped and wouldn’t pay attention to your offering unless they are convinced that the event will bring significant value. Thus, acquiring top executives for a virtual event requires a laser focussed approach.

Creating a customized database of the targeted audience followed by a strategic email outreach campaign is the key to connect with your prospects. Timely follow-up emails will help you in keeping the audiences hooked to be able to turn them into attendees. A content syndication campaign can boost your chances to reach out to the executives.
B2B Sales Arrow recommends four key points to take on board while trying to acquire a C-Suite audience for your virtual event.

The C-Suite executives are always hard-pressed for time. So, if you want to gain their trust and get them to register for your event, you must consider a registration process that is easy to navigate, with a distraction-free landing page design and crystal clear content. The top executives are always conscious about sharing their data. Therefore, you must make an effort to assure your audience of data security, with end-to-end data encryption.

Incorporating strong visual elements into your communication can improve your chances to connect with your C-Suite prospects. A Quartz study shows that 66% of the top executives found data visualizations a major draw to the content, followed by photography (52%) and charts (42%).

You must strive to bring on board, industry heavyweights and eminent thought leaders as keynote speakers and panelists. Besides being a value builder as they share the latest trends and technological development within a specific industry, their presence also renders the much-needed X-factor that draws a C-Suite audience to your event.

In a virtual setting, it wouldn’t be wrong to say that your content is the soul of your event. Your event agenda is your first inducement and so, it must be customized specifically to your C-Suite audience. Top executives are always in pursuit of ideas that are intriguing and useful. They fancy value-based solutions that address the challenges their industry is facing currently and are highly concerned with their overall outcome and impact. The information must have credible sources and must avoid or minimize any trace of a sales pitch, else, your audience will shy away from the event.

It is recommended that every detail of the campaign be tailored to connect with each target’s name, designation, function, geography, etc. Personalized URLs could be one way of achieving this level of customization. A microsite carrying the top executive’s name, such as www.YourEventName.com is most likely to leave a deeper impression on the recipient. Hyper personalization has the potential to drive one-on-one conversation.
With a virtual event, just because you can’t shake hands with your peers, your opportunity for networking doesn’t disappear.

The C-Suite executives deeply value the opportunity to connect with peers and experts, but they also expect quality assurance on the fellow attendees, i.e., they are keen to know the guests and speakers’ profile – their line of business, the positions they hold, etc. One CEO will accept the invitation, only once they know their peers are already joining the event. Thus, inviting key participants from the outset not only elevates the standard of the event but also makes it easier for you to get the initial few CEOs on board.

The bottom line is that to have a bigger impact, you should acquire the C-Suite audience for your virtual events and expect their participation in your future events. Nevertheless, there are no shortcuts to achieving this. It is paramount that you develop a highly personalized and meaningful agenda, provide them with high-quality speakers and networking opportunities, backed by clear-cut communication all throughout. A well-thought-out strategy with the aforementioned ideas will help you captivate this elusive set of audiences.

title: Knowledgeable Session on Covid-19 -
excerpt: Unfortunately, at present when everyone seems to be in a sheer dilemma of what to do, which vaccine to get, […]

body.text: Unfortunately, at present when everyone seems to be in a sheer dilemma of what to do, which vaccine to get, hospital or home quarantine, and many more such doubts, we are already losing people from amongst us, in a snap of the finger. Before you understand, someone is just gone!! Sadly, we have seen the demise of 3 teammates’ family members in the last week itself. This has made us deeply concerned about the current situation, where we directly cannot do anything but yes, we can at least guide the team upon the current scenario.

Team #B2BSalesArrow is very thankful to honorable Dr. Yogendra Lohani and Dr. Lalita Lohani carrying four decades plus experience in the medical field, for taking out time and enlightening us and broadening the sphere of our knowledge on the current Covid-19 scenario in our Saturday Health Session on 8 th May 2021.

Starting from best practices and correct usage of Mask, they guided us upon the importance of the vaccine, which Yogasan and exercises should be done, diet to be followed, keeping ourselves safe from and cleared many myths of the team with respect to Covid-19, Vaccine, and Tests. They also guided upon few special case scenarios.
Few valuable insights from the same session: –

Vaccination : It is safe and an absolute must! Getting a vaccine will build the immunity in 4-6 weeks, both the shots are mandatory for effective coverage. Wave 3 will soon hit all of us again, Children will also be impacted. The only way out is getting vaccinated.

Yoga and Exercise : It has been proven good to perform some or the other physical exercises like Walking, or Yogasans like Kapal Bhati, Anulom Vilom, Bhastrika to improve the respiratory system and give strength to the lungs. Also, lying/sleeping face down helps the posterior lungs expand and increase the flow of oxygen.
Basic Equipments : Pulse Oximeter, Thermometer, and a few antibiotics and medicines are advised to keep handy for a quick check on the situation.

Precautions when tested positive: After testing positive, isolate yourself immediately. Monitor your Oxygen level and temperature every 3-4 hours and connect with an Expert immediately if the Oxygen level dips below 92 or the temperature rises above 100 Degrees.
Diet: Take a high protein, less oily, low on carbs diet. Add in plenty of fresh fruits and raw vegetables. Avoid taking non-vegetarian food.
Let us hope to come out of this gloomy situation very soon and can meet each other in person and not virtually.
Thank you once again to Dr. Yogendra Lohani and Dr. Lalita Lohani for the deep insights. Reference URLs:
1.) https://www.who.int/emergencies/diseases/novel-coronavirus-2019/question-and-answers-hub/q-a-detail/coronavirus-disease-covid-19
2.) https://www.icmr.gov.in/
